description: The link picker, image picker, and upload dialog are part of Hydra's chrome — they look the same on every site. What varies by design system is which links and images are click-to-edit in the preview: a site might wire up every link inline, or only a few "primary" links, with everything else editable from the sidebar. Same applies to images.

parent.description: This guide is for content editors using a Hydra-powered site. It covers how to use the editor — how to select things, edit text, add and move blocks, work with containers and templates — without assuming you know how the site was built.
parent.title: Editor Guide

title: Links and media

p-1.plaintext: The link picker, image picker, and upload dialog are part of Hydra's chrome — they look the same on every site. What varies by design system is **which** links and images are click-to-edit in the preview: a site might wire up every link inline, or only a few "primary" links, with everything else editable from the sidebar. Same applies to images.

children.text: The link picker, image picker, and upload dialog are part of Hydra's chrome — they look the same on every site. What varies by design system is
which
links and images are click-to-edit in the preview: a site might wire up every link inline, or only a few "primary" links, with everything else editable from the sidebar. Same applies to images.
Editing a link

p-3.plaintext: When the frontend has wired up a link field as inline-editable, clicking the link in the preview doesn't navigate — it opens the **link picker** instead. From the picker you can:

children.text: When the frontend has wired up a link field as inline-editable, clicking the link in the preview doesn't navigate — it opens the
link picker
instead. From the picker you can:

ul-4.plaintext: Pick a CMS page by browsing the content tree. Type/paste an external URL. Open the URL in a new tab (toggle the "Open in new tab" option). Clear the link.

children.text: Pick a CMS page
by browsing the content tree.
Type/paste an external URL.
Open the URL in a new tab
(toggle the "Open in new tab" option).
Clear the link.

img-5.alt: Link picker on a slate link — URL field with /another-page, browse / clear / open-new-tab / submit icons.

p-6.plaintext: The Quanta toolbar's link icon does the same thing and is available on slate text fields too — select some text, click the link icon, and the link picker opens for that text range.

children.text: The Quanta toolbar's link icon does the same thing and is available on slate text fields too — select some text, click the link icon, and the link picker opens for that text range.
Uploading media

p-8.plaintext: When the frontend marks an image (or other media element) as inline-editable, you can:

children.text: When the frontend marks an image (or other media element) as inline-editable, you can:
Empty media element

p-10.plaintext: You'll see an empty placeholder with a prompt to **upload, browse, or drag in** an image. Three ways:

children.text: You'll see an empty placeholder with a prompt to
upload, browse, or drag in
an image. Three ways:

ul-11.plaintext: Click the placeholder → the media picker opens. Pick from existing CMS images or upload a new one. Drag an image file from your desktop and drop it directly onto the placeholder. Drag an image from another tab / source if your browser supports it.

children.text: Click the placeholder → the media picker opens. Pick from existing CMS images or upload a new one.
Drag an image file from your desktop and drop it directly onto the placeholder.
Drag an image from another tab / source if your browser supports it.

img-12.alt: Empty image block selected — placeholder with image icon, "Browse the site, drop an image..." input, sidebar showing "NO IMAGE SELECTED".

children.text: Replacing an existing media element

p-14.plaintext: Hover the image — controls appear. You can:

children.text: Hover the image — controls appear. You can:

ul-15.plaintext: Replace — opens the media picker to pick or upload a different image. Remove — clears the field, returns to the empty placeholder state. Drag and drop a new image directly onto the existing one to replace it.

children.text: Replace
— opens the media picker to pick or upload a different image.
Remove
— clears the field, returns to the empty placeholder state.
Drag and drop a new image
directly onto the existing one to replace it.

p-16.plaintext: The same actions are available from the sidebar field if you'd rather not click into the preview.

children.text: The same actions are available from the sidebar field if you'd rather not click into the preview.
What gets stored

p-18.plaintext: When you pick or upload an image, what gets stored on the block isn't a URL string — it's a small object containing the image's CMS path, the field name, and the available scales. The frontend resolves a specific scale at render time (so the same image data renders at thumbnail size in a listing and full size in a hero). You don't have to think about scales as an editor; they're a developer concern.

children.text: When you pick or upload an image, what gets stored on the block isn't a URL string — it's a small object containing the image's CMS path, the field name, and the available scales. The frontend resolves a specific scale at render time (so the same image data renders at thumbnail size in a listing and full size in a hero). You don't have to think about scales as an editor; they're a developer concern.
What's not (yet) inline-editable

p-20.plaintext: A few media types currently still require sidebar editing:

children.text: A few media types currently still require sidebar editing:

ul-21.plaintext: File uploads (PDFs, downloads) — pickable from the sidebar but no drag-onto-preview support yet. Embedded video URLs — sidebar field; click-to-edit on the player isn't wired up.

children.text: File uploads
(PDFs, downloads) — pickable from the sidebar but no drag-onto-preview support yet.
Embedded video URLs
— sidebar field; click-to-edit on the player isn't wired up.

p-22.plaintext: If your frontend doesn't expose a particular media field as inline-editable, you can always edit it from the sidebar — that's available for every field.

children.text: If your frontend doesn't expose a particular media field as inline-editable, you can always edit it from the sidebar — that's available for every field.
